Package neqsim.process.safety.dispersion

Class ProbitModel

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.safety.dispersion.ProbitModel
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class ProbitModel extends Object implements Serializable
Probit (probability unit) consequence model for fatality / injury fraction from a hazard dose.

Computes Y = a + b · ln(D), then converts the probit Y to a fatality fraction via the standard normal CDF: P = 0.5 · (1 + erf((Y − 5) / √2)). The dose D depends on the hazard:

  • Toxic: D = ∫ C^n dt with C in ppm, t in min (varies by chemical)
  • Thermal: D = ∫ I^(4/3) dt with I in W/m², t in s
  • Overpressure: D = ΔP in Pa (or with impulse for structural damage)

References: CCPS Yellow Book / Eisenberg, Lynch & Breeding (1975) / TNO Green Book.

Version:
1.0
Author:
ESOL

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    ProbitModel(double a, double b, double n)
    Construct a probit model with constants Y = a + b · ln(D^n) ≡ a + b·n·ln(D).

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static ProbitModel
    ammoniaFatality()
    Toxic-fatality probit for ammonia (TNO Green Book).
    static ProbitModel
    blastLungFatality()
    Lung-haemorrhage fatality probit for blast overpressure (Eisenberg): Y = -77.1 + 6.91·ln(ΔP), ΔP in Pa.
    static ProbitModel
    chlorineFatality()
    Toxic-fatality probit for chlorine (Eisenberg): Y = -17.10 + 1.69·ln(C^2.75·t), C in ppm, t in min.
    private static double
    erf(double x)
     
    static ProbitModel
    h2sFatality()
    Toxic-fatality probit for H₂S (TNO Green Book): Y = -31.42 + 3.008·ln(C^1.43·t), C in ppm, t in min.

    Convert probit Y to fatality / injury fraction P (0..1).

    Direct dose → probability shortcut.
    double
    probit(double dose)
    Compute the probit Y for a given dose.
    static ProbitModel
    thermalFatality()
    Thermal radiation fatality probit for unprotected skin (Eisenberg): Y = -14.9 + 2.56·ln(t·I^(4/3)/10⁴), t in s, I in W/m².

  • Constructor Details

    • ProbitModel

      public ProbitModel(double a, double b, double n)
      Construct a probit model with constants Y = a + b · ln(D^n) ≡ a + b·n·ln(D). Many sources publish (a, b) with n already absorbed; in that case set n = 1.
      Parameters:
      a - probit intercept
      b - probit slope
      n - dose exponent (use 1 if (a,b) already include it)

  • Method Details

    • probit

      public double probit(double dose)
      Compute the probit Y for a given dose.
      Parameters:
      dose - dose value (units depend on hazard)
      Returns:
      probit value Y

    • probability

      public double probability(double Y)
      Convert probit Y to fatality / injury fraction P (0..1).
      Parameters:
      Y - probit value
      Returns:
      probability in [0, 1]

    • probabilityFromDose

      public double probabilityFromDose(double dose)
      Direct dose → probability shortcut.
      Parameters:
      dose - hazard-specific dose
      Returns:
      probability in [0, 1]

    • erf

      private static double erf(double x)

    • h2sFatality

      public static ProbitModel h2sFatality()
      Toxic-fatality probit for H₂S (TNO Green Book): Y = -31.42 + 3.008·ln(C^1.43·t), C in ppm, t in min.
      Returns:
      ProbitModel for H₂S fatality

      public static ProbitModel thermalFatality()
      Thermal radiation fatality probit for unprotected skin (Eisenberg): Y = -14.9 + 2.56·ln(t·I^(4/3)/10⁴), t in s, I in W/m².
      Returns:
      ProbitModel for thermal radiation fatality

    • blastLungFatality

      public static ProbitModel blastLungFatality()
      Lung-haemorrhage fatality probit for blast overpressure (Eisenberg): Y = -77.1 + 6.91·ln(ΔP), ΔP in Pa.
      Returns:
      ProbitModel for blast lung damage fatality
